uk: Неділя 7-ма по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 6. Єв. 7.
saints.en: 6th Sunday after Pentecost. Sts. Martyrs Proclus & Hilary. Ven. Michael of Malea

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» 10 стихир: 7 воскрес., 3 мчч. , Сл.: мчч., І нині: догмат 6-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня. Стиховня неділ. По «Отче наш» тропар воскрес., Сл.: тр. мчч., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом тр. мчч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь» тропар воскрес., Сл.: мчч., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом Сл. Катизми черг., сідал. неділ., «Ангельський собор», іпакой, степенна та прокімен 6-го гласу. Єв. – Ів. 63 зач.; 20, 1-10. «Воскресіння Христове». Пс. 50. Стихира неділ. Канон воскресний, хрестовоскресний, богород. і мчч. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». По 3-ій – конд.-ікос мчч., сідал. мчч. без богород., Сл., І нині: богород. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос неділ. На 9-ій – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світил. неділ., Сл.: світил. мчч., І нині: богород. неділ. світил. Хвалитних стихир 8 неділ., Сл.: стихира євангел., І нині: «Преблагословенна». По вел. славослов'ї тр. «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий з помин. Служби неділ. і мчч.
Часи: на всіх – тропар воскресний, Слава: на 3-му і 9-му – тр. мчч., на 6-му – храму. Кондаки: на 1-му – неділ., на 3-му і 9-му – мчч., на 6-му храму.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Сл.: кондак, І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом Сл. Прокімен і алилуя воскресні. Ап. – Рим. 116 зач.; 15, 1-7. Єв. – Мт. 33 зач.; 9, 27-35. Причасний воскресний.

troparion_en.text: Your holy martyrs Proclus and Hilary, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “As You were voluntarily raised...”)
Like the morning star, / your glorious sufferings enlighten us with holy miracles. / We celebrate your memory, Proclus and Hilary; / pray to Christ God for us / that He will save our souls.

matched_oca_saint: Martyrs Proclus and Hilary of Ancyra

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Proclus and Hilary of Ancyra

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Proclus and Hilary, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “As You were voluntarily raised...”)
Like the morning star, / your glorious sufferings enlighten us with holy miracles. / We celebrate your memory, Proclus and Hilary; / pray to Christ God for us / that He will save our souls.

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away, / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, O holy Father Michael, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text: By your deeds you withered the arrogance of the flesh; / through enlightenment you gave wings to your agility of spirit. / You were revealed as a dwelling place of the Trinity, / Whom you now clearly behold. / Blessed Michael, unceasingly pray for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Theodore and his son, John, of Kiev

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Theodore and John, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Simon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Golinduc (in Baptism Mary), of Persia

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Mary, O Lord, / through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through her intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Mary;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable John the Georgian of Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father John, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Gabriel the Georgian of Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Gabriel, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God “of the Three Hands” on Mount Athos

troparion.text: From Palestine, the godly-minded Savva / brought us your venerable icon, O holy Bride of God, / which is known as the icon “Of the Three Hands”; / and now, since the monks of Hilandar possess it as their boast, / they send up hymns of praise and thanksgiving, / crying out in exultation: / Rejoice, O full of grace!

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “O victorious Leader...”)Come, let us revere the famed icon of the Queen of All, / known as the icon “Of the Three Hands” because of the marvel which it wrought; / and with fervent faith and longing let us cry out, / “O pure maiden, preserve your monastery and your flock from all dangers, / tribulations and adversities, for we cry to you: / Rejoice, O help and defense of all!”

oca_saints.name: Saint Veronica (Bernice), the woman with the issue of blood

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Mother, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away, / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, O Holy Mother Veronica, rejoices with the Angels!

Your holy martyr Serapion, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Paisios of the Holy Mountain

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “O citizen of the desert...”)
O you faithful, let us honor Paϊsios, the offspring of Phárasa,   / the glory of Athos, emulator of the Holy Ascetics of old, and equal to them in honor;  /  O grace-filled vessel, who hastens speedily to the pious faithful who cry out:  /  “Glory to Him Who strengthened you.   / Glory to Him Who crowned you.  /  Glory to Him Who, through you, works healings for all.”

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
Let us praise the most renowned ascetic of the Holy Mountain, /  and the newly-shining luminary of the Church;  / let us praise him with hymns with all our heart,  / for he guides the faithful toward a life of excellence, filling them with rivers of gifts; /  therefore we cry out: “Rejoice, Father Paϊsios.”
